articles.title: Rajasthan faces rain, strong winds and heatwave: Sri Ganganagar records highest temperature at 45.6°C; weat... - Bhaskar English
articles.full_text:
Rajasthan faces rain, strong winds and heatwave:Sri Ganganagar records highest temperature at 45.6°C; weather shift likely from June 11
Rain and heatwave alerts have been issued for Rajasthan on Tuesday. Today, there is a possibility of thunderstorms and rain in 7 districts. Meanwhile, a heatwave warning has been issued for 5 districts.
Meanwhile, a new Western Disturbance will become active from June 11. Due to its effect, the spell of thunderstorms and rain is likely to resume in the state.
Before this, Monday saw intense heat and humidity. Even amidst the pre-monsoon, the mercury is continuously rising. Temperatures in Sriganganagar, Jaisalmer, and Bikaner were recorded between 44 and 46 degrees Celsius. Sriganganagar was the hottest city in the country with a temperature of 45.6 degrees.
These cities were the hottest
Temperature in major cities of Rajasthan
Sri Ganganagar hottest at 45.6°C, several Rajasthan cities cross 40°C mark
Major updates on heat and rain
Weather in the last 24 hours: The weather remained clear in most cities of Rajasthan on Monday. Temperatures rose due to sunshine throughout the day. On Monday, the maximum temperature in Barmer, Bikaner, Churu, Jaipur, Jaisalmer, Sriganganagar, Kota, and Jodhpur ranged between 40 and 46 degrees Celsius.
Along with intense heat, these cities also experienced humidity. According to the Meteorological Department, Sriganganagar in Rajasthan was the hottest city in the country. The temperature recorded here was 45.6 degrees.
New System to become Active: In most areas of Jodhpur and Bikaner divisions of Western Rajasthan, the weather is likely to remain completely clear for the next 2-3 days, with a possibility of temperature rise by 2-3 degrees Celsius.
Heatwave conditions are possible in some places. With another new Western Disturbance becoming active from June 11, there is a possibility of thunderstorms and rain in some parts of the state.
.
